rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical amino-oxygenation cyclization of aryl alkenes with sulfonamide-tethered alcohol bisnucleophiles to form saturated 6-, 7-, and 8-membered N/O-heterocycles (morpholines, 1,4-oxazepanes, 1,4-oxazocanes).
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the aryl alkene to an alkene radical cation; cathodic reduction of protons to H2; no redox mediator and undivided cell.

key_intermediates.name: N-centered sulfonamide anion (I) from DBU deprotonation of N-(2-hydroxyethyl)benzenesulfonamide 1a
Aryl alkene radical cation (II) from anodic oxidation of 2b (alpha-methylstyrene)
Carbon-centered radical III from N-anion addition to radical cation II
Carbocation IV from further anodic oxidation of radical III
Cyclized morpholine product 4ab after intramolecular O-attack and deprotonation

electron_flow: DBU deprotonates the sulfonamide to give an N-centered anion (I); the anode oxidizes the aryl alkene (2b) to an alkene radical cation (II); I attacks II to give carbon-centered radical III with high regioselectivity; III is oxidized at the anode to cation IV; the tethered hydroxyl of 1a closes onto IV with proton release to form the saturated N/O-heterocycle, while H2 evolves at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: HFIP (added cosolvent / oxidation probe)
probe_reagents.observation: HFIP lowers oxidation potential of alkene 2b; with DBU+HFIP the DBU-induced lowering of sulfonamide oxidation is diminished, suggesting alkene is preferentially oxidized.

probe_reagents.name: DBU (base)
probe_reagents.observation: Effectively reduces onset oxidation potential of sulfonamide 1a (CV), consistent with deprotonation to N-anion.

probe_reagents.name: Na2CO3 (base in SWV control)
probe_reagents.observation: Base significantly facilitates formation of intermediate III from I + II coupling without electrode SET.

probe_reagents.name: 1a + 2b mixing (SWV coupling test)
probe_reagents.observation: Adding 1a to 2b under SWV decreases the 2b oxidation current, indicating chemical coupling between N-anion I and radical cation II rather than independent SET.

notes: Key mechanistic support comes from CV/SWV (Figure 4) showing onset potentials 1a 2.21 V, 2b 1.46 V, 6b 1.67 V (vs Ag/AgCl) — alkene is oxidized preferentially — plus in situ EC-APCI-MS (Figure 5) detecting protonated intermediate III ([M+H]+ m/z 333.1393) and product 4ab ([M+H]+ m/z 332.1315) with real-time EIC profiles showing III rising then declining as 4ab forms. An alternative sulfonamide-N-radical addition pathway (oxidation of 1a first) is ruled out because HFIP/DBU experiments and lower potential of 2b indicate the alkene radical cation, not the N-radical, is the key oxidized species.
